Step 3 – Placing and Connecting the Cuff

Use either a SunTech Orbit-K™ blood pressure cuff or a SunTech Single Patient Use kit (containing a disposable blood pressure cuff and microphone pad). This section gives directions for proper size selection and placement of either style of cuff. It is

important that the cuff is properly fitted to the patient’s arm, and that the microphone is placed over the brachial artery (between the bicep and tricep)! Improper cuff sizing and a misplaced microphone can lead to missed or poor readings and accuracy.
Orbit-K Cuff Single Patient Use Kit
a. Locate the brachial artery on the left arm. a. Locate the brachial artery on the left arm.
b. Slide the cuff sleeve up the patient’s arm, with the “ARTERY” marker pointing down the arm. b. Place the microphone onto the microphone pad. Peel the protective film from the microphone pad.

c. There is a microphone located under the “ARTERY” marker. Make sure the microphone is placed on the inner portion of the arm, directly over the Note: the single patient use kit uses the microphone from the Orbit-K cuff.

brachial artery between the bicep & tricep. There should be about 3 to 5 cm (two finger widths) between the edge of the cuff and the elbow. c. Place the microphone on the patient’s arm making sure that the microphone is placed on the medial part of the arm, directly over the brachial artery

d. Insert the 3-pin microphone connector from the cuff into the corresponding connector on the Patient Cable. The connector can be inserted in any between the bicep and tricep. There should be about 3 to 5 cm (two finger widths) between the microphone pad and the elbow.
orientation. d. Wrap the cuff around the arm and secure.

e. Connect the tube from the cuff into the corresponding connector on the Patient Cable and twist. e. Insert the 3-pin microphone connector from the cuff into the corresponding connector on the Patient Cable. The connectors can be inserted in any
